admissible_options.description: The model emits validated arithmetic intent and operands; repository code performs the calculation and returns a typed result.
admissible_options.name: typed_deterministic_action_realization

admissible_options.description: Bind a different model and rerun a newly authorized capability experiment under a new evidence lineage.
admissible_options.name: new_model_capability_binding

adr_decision_boundary.required_decision: Choose the next system boundary for deterministic arithmetic reliability without rewriting the failed evidence.

alternative_hypotheses.evidence: Planned, API, and metric prompt-token counts match exactly for all three observed requests.
Payment turn 1 passed JSON parsing, exact key set, case ID, turn index, confidence, no-extra-text, and schema checks.
alternative_hypotheses.hypothesis: The JSON schema or output envelope failed.

alternative_hypotheses.evidence: Payment turn 1 passed telemetry and cold-cache gates; the failure code was OUTPUT_ANSWER_MISMATCH.
alternative_hypotheses.hypothesis: Cache telemetry caused the payment failure.

alternative_hypotheses.evidence: Observed requests used worker_1 as authorized; cleanup was clean and both ports closed.
alternative_hypotheses.hypothesis: Route realization or worker lifecycle failed.

alternative_hypotheses.evidence: The ledger retained the failed request metadata and the zero-failure policy aborted without retries or replacements.
alternative_hypotheses.hypothesis: The harness hid or retried the failed answer.

alternative_hypotheses.evidence: The arithmetic case failed exact-answer matching after all observed harness gates passed.
alternative_hypotheses.hypothesis: The pinned model and frozen prompt contract did not reliably satisfy the mixed semantic canary.

alternative_hypotheses.evidence: Incident-severity turn 2 showed positive cache reuse. The remaining cache observations were not executed.
alternative_hypotheses.hypothesis: Cache-aware routing as a whole failed.

claims.statement: The prior rendered-token counting defect is remediated for all three observed requests.
Positive same-worker turn-two cache reuse is qualified for the incident-severity trajectory only.
The terminal failure occurred at the model-output semantic answer boundary, after the observed harness boundaries passed.
The evidence is consistent with insufficient arithmetic or instruction-execution capability under the pinned model and frozen prompt contract, but does not isolate a lower-level internal model cause.
Payment-reconciliation turn 2 and both data-sharing-policy turns are NOT_OBSERVED.
The six-request canary failed under its frozen all-or-nothing acceptance contract.
The v2 rerun authorization is consumed and cannot be reused.
The full 72-trajectory measured benchmark remains blocked.

counterexample.conclusion: At least one required request produced a semantically incorrect answer; therefore the all-six canary cannot pass.
counterexample.required_for: claim that the mixed six-request canary qualified

definitions.text: The bounded canary passes only if all three two-turn trajectories complete, all six requests pass quality, schema, telemetry, cache, route, and zero-failure gates, and cleanup is clean.
A request is observed only when a ledger record exists. Unexecuted requests are NOT_OBSERVED and cannot be labeled passed, failed, zero-cache, or positive-cache.

A harness defect is supported when repository planning, validation, routing, telemetry, or scoring diverges from the frozen contract. A model-output semantic failure is supported when those boundaries pass but the deterministic expected answer does not match.
The rerun authorization is consumed once real canary model requests execute; it cannot authorize another run.

formal_conclusion.answer: The v2.3 canary did not qualify. The observed terminal failure is an exact semantic answer mismatch under an otherwise passing observed harness path.
derivation: By D1, all six requests must pass.
By P6, payment-reconciliation turn 1 failed exact-answer matching.
By P7, execution aborted after three requests and three remained not observed.
By C1, the original token-counting defect is not the terminal cause.
By C3, the terminal divergence is at the model-output semantic answer boundary.
By C7, the authorization is consumed.
By C8, the full measured benchmark remains blocked.

method_basis.adaptation: execution-grounded premise -> trace -> divergence -> alternative-hypothesis -> conclusion certificate
method_basis.name: semi-formal reasoning
method_basis.source: Ugare and Chandra, Agentic Code Reasoning, arXiv:2603.01896v1

next_safe_action: Review and approve this certificate, then draft the ADR using only the admissible decision boundary above. Keep GPU execution off.
non_claims: The data-sharing-policy case passed or failed.
All case types benefit from cache reuse.
The pinned model is universally incapable of arithmetic.
A larger model will solve the problem.
Deterministic action realization is implemented or validated.

premises.statement: Repository, audit, authorization, token-normalization, case-manifest, model, and runtime bindings qualified.
The frozen scope was three Condition-C trajectories, six requests, worker_1 -> worker_1, full restart before each trajectory, no retries, and no replacements.
Incident-severity turn 1 passed exact output quality, schema, telemetry, route, and cold-cache gates; planned, API, and metric prompt-token counts were all 282.
Incident-severity turn 2 passed exact output quality, schema, telemetry, route, and positive-cache gates; 192 cached tokens were observed from 205 eligible shared prefix tokens.
Payment-reconciliation turn 1 passed HTTP, JSON parsing, exact key set, case ID, turn index, confidence, schema, telemetry, route, and cold-cache gates.
Payment-reconciliation turn 1 failed only the exact-answer check and emitted OUTPUT_ANSWER_MISMATCH.
The zero-failure policy aborted after the third completed request; payment turn 2 and both data-sharing-policy turns were not executed.
Both workers exited cleanly and ports 8001 and 8002 closed.

question: What does the v2.3 canary evidence prove about the failure, which alternative explanations are refuted, and what decision boundary may the ADR address?
title: AuraGateway Local A/B/C Schema-Constrained Quality/Cache Canary Rerun v2.3
